sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Low back pain with right leg tingling.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports low back pain that began approximately six hours ago after raking leaves in the yard. He describes pins and needles in his right foot and reports tightness in his lower back. He denies prior similar episodes and reports no pain on extension but some on flexion and palpation around the L5 region. The patient's partner reports experiencing similar pain when the patient is standing. He has not taken any medication for the pain.
Findings
Physical examination reveals normal dorsiflexion and equal pulses bilaterally. The patient reports tingling in his right leg. An x-ray of the low back revealed no acute abnormalities. The patient had an MRI performed previously, and no surgery has been performed.
Assessment
The patient's presentation is consistent with a lumbar strain.
Plan
I will prescribe meloxicam and physical therapy to address the lumbar strain. I have ordered an MRI to evaluate for any further complications. Follow up in two weeks if symptoms persist, and instruct the patient to contact the office if symptoms worsen.
